mission.title: redpanda: Handle unsupported topic configurations as no-op via an allowlist
mission.description: Handle unsupported topic configurations as no-op via an allowlist. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current redpanda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your redpanda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
helm list -n redpanda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working redpanda installation.

steps.title: Review redpanda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant redpanda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl get configmap -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=redpanda
```
### Who is this for and what problem do they have today?

Existing Kafka API clients that configure topics with properties that are not relevant for Redpanda.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Handle unsupported topic configurations as no-op via an…
steps.description:
## Cover letter

Skip topic configuration propeties that are not relevant for Redpanda, but used to configure Kafka

Added allowlist for all Kafka properties that are unsupported in Redpanda

### Improvements

* Alter topic configuration not fail if gets unsupported properties

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl get events -n redpanda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Handle unsupported topic configurations as no-op via an…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Cover letter

Skip topic configuration propeties that are not relevant for Redpanda, but used to configure Kafka

Added allowlist for all Kafka properties that are unsupported in Redpanda

### Improvements

* Alter topic configuration not fail if gets unsupported properties

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with redpanda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
